channel.title: JLT Pillar Posts — LinkedIn
channel.description: In-depth pillar posts from JLT Group — authority content for the trade

item.title: The UK home-based travel agent sector has grown substantially over the past decade. With that growth has come a clearer need to distinguish between two genuinely different models operating under similar descriptions.
item.description:
The UK home-based travel agent sector has grown substantially over the past decade. With that growth has come a clearer need to distinguish between two genuinely different models operating under similar descriptions.
A legitimate host travel agency is built around the holiday. Agents earn from the bookings they take, supported by real supplier relationships, ATOL protection, and a structured training pathway.
A different model is built around recruitment. Holidays may feature, but income flows from downlines, team size, and earnings that move upward through a hierarchy.
The practical test is a single question: "If I never recruit anyone and only sell holidays, what does my income look like?" A credible host agency answers that plainly.
Within JLT, agents earn from their own bookings, there are no recruitment quotas, and the cost is a one-off joining fee plus one transparent monthly membership fee, covering the booking platform, supplier access, and the JLT Academy.
The full article sets out the compensation model and five common red flags.
Blog link to be added in the first comment after 24-48 hrs:
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/travel-agent-from-home-mlm-how-to-tell
